# Corpus v0.2 — J0 scoping pass (measure before crawling)

*Workstream J0. Generated by `ml/corpus/report_v0_2_scoping.py` from the measurement sidecars under `data/interim/j0/` (untracked). Token unit is **kreyol-bpe** (the training tokenizer — the same unit as the "112M unique tokens" v0.1 figure); o200k is shown alongside where it matters. Spec: [../../docs/data.md](../../docs/data.md) §1–§2.*

## The binding red-flag gate

docs/data.md J0 sets a **STOP-and-report** gate before the multi-hour VOA crawl (J1) and the rest of the workstream:

> STOP after J0 if any of: **VOA measured net-new < 5M tokens**, or the **wire-content rate makes clean filtering infeasible (>40% of articles)**, or **robots.txt blocks the crawl**.

### Determination: **RED FLAG — STOP after J0**

| Criterion | Threshold | Measured | Trips? |
|---|---|--:|:--:|
| VOA net-new tokens | ≥ 5M | **2.25M kreyol-bpe** (2.88M o200k) | 🚩 **YES** |
| Wire-content rate | ≤ 40% of articles | 33.9% | no |
| robots.txt | must allow `/a/` | allows (no crawl-delay) | no |

VOA — the survey's *one big unlock* for **authored journalism** — delivers only **~2.25M net-new kreyol-bpe tokens**, not the estimated 20–60M. The *authored-share* premise of v0.2 does not survive measurement. A separate surprise cuts the other way: **fineweb-2 hat is far larger than the survey assumed** (§2) — real volume, but the same web-crawl register. The two findings pull in opposite directions; see §5 for what it means and the recommendation.

## 1. VOA Nouvèl (voanouvel.com)

**robots.txt** (fetched 2026-07-23): article paths `/a/` are **allowed** (Disallow covers only /z/, /tv/, /radio/, /schedule/, search, comments, embeds, podcast sublinks). No `Crawl-delay` → we self-impose ~1 req/s. Cloudflare-fronted; normal browser headers suffice (a naive fetch 403s).

**Sitemap enumeration.** Two gzipped article shards (`sitemap_413_1/2.xml.gz`) list **39,286 article URLs**, all under `/a/`, spanning **2002 → 2026-07** (by `lastmod`). A small block of the newest IDs (~150) 302-redirect to a topic page and are skipped.

**Sample.** Stratified-by-year sample of **202 URLs** (seed 20260724), fetched at ~1 req/s and extracted (headline + `div.intro` lede + `div.wsw` body).

### The article funnel (why 39k URLs ≠ 39k articles)

| stage | sample | rate | projected on 39,286 URLs |
|---|--:|--:|--:|
| sitemap URLs | 202 | 100% | 39,286 |
| — live **text** articles | 109 | 54.0% | ~21,199 |
| — of which **wire** (AFP/AP/Reuters, dropped) | 37 | 33.9% of articles | — |
| — **PD-clean** articles (kept) | 72 | 35.6% of URLs | ~14,003 |

The other **46% of `/a/` URLs are non-text**: daily radio program pages (*Pwogram maten / mitan / aprè-midi*, *Bonjou Ayiti*, *Randevou-Radyo*, *Edisyon Maten*) and video clips — VOA Nouvèl is a broadcast outlet, so roughly half its article URLs carry an audio/video player and no prose body.

**Wire carve-out.** 33.9% of text articles are bylined to a wire agency (AFP, AP=Associated Press, Reuters) — licensed material, **not** VOA public domain — and are dropped whole. (Editorial *mentions* of a wire agency in VOA-written copy are correctly kept; only the byline triggers the drop.)

**Length.** PD-clean articles are short: mean **295** / median 252 kreyol-bpe tokens (max 1157).

**MADLAD overlap.** MADLAD (Common Crawl) already scraped voanouvel.com, so **31.9% of PD-clean articles (45.5% of their tokens) near-duplicate documents already in v0.1** (MinHash 5-gram, Jaccard ≥ 0.8; n=72).

### Net-new projection

| unit | gross PD-clean | − MADLAD overlap | **net-new** |
|---|--:|--:|--:|
| kreyol-bpe | 4.13M | 45.5% | **2.25M** |
| o200k | 5.29M | 45.5% | **2.88M** |

Even at the sample's 95% CI upper bound the net-new stays under 5M. **VOA ≈ 2.25M net-new kreyol-bpe tokens of authored journalism** — real and register-unique, but ~10–25× below estimate.

## 2. fineweb-2 `hat_Latn`

Streamed the full `hat_Latn` train split (**224,472 docs / 159.29M kreyol-bpe tokens raw**), then applied the standing langid + junk filters and MinHash-deduped against v0.1:

| gate | docs dropped | note |
|---|--:|---|
| langid (fineweb hat score < 0.65) | 3,038 | |
| junk fingerprints | 4,700 | {'price_listing': 2948, 'commercial_spam': 832, 'foreign_script': 107, 'mt_placeholder': 813} |
| MinHash dup vs v0.1 | 37,942 | MADLAD overlap |
| **net-new** | — | **178,792 docs / 108.49M kreyol-bpe** (68% of raw) |

**This contradicts the survey.** docs/data.md expected "heavy MADLAD overlap … not new volume"; measured, only **16.9% of docs** dedup against v0.1 and **68% of raw tokens survive** as net-new. MADLAD-400 (2023 snapshots) and fineweb-2 (through 2024, different extraction/dedup) share few *text*-level near-duplicates even where source URLs overlap, so fineweb-2 is a **large new web-crawl source (~108.49M kreyol-bpe)** — nearly double the whole v0.1 corpus. Caveat: MinHash measures text near-dup, so this is net-new *text*; semantically it is the same translationese web register as MADLAD, not a new register.

## 3. Bloom Library (`sil-ai/bloom-lm` hat)

**Gated (auto-grant).** The dataset requires agreeing to share contact info; the token account has not yet accepted. Access is auto-granted on one click at huggingface.co/datasets/sil-ai/bloom-lm — **pending a click on the user's HF account** (the browser session was not connected during J0). Structure confirmed from the public card: fields `text`/`license`/`title`; 260 hat stories; licenses cc-by-4.0 / cc-by-nc-4.0 / cc-by-nd-4.0 / cc-by-sa. Keeper count + tokens will be finalized once access is granted (`corpus/j0_bloom.py` is ready to run).

## 4. Estimate vs measured (docs/data.md §1)

| source | docs/data.md estimate | measured (J0) | status |
|---|---|---|---|
| **VOA Nouvèl** | ~20–60M | **~2.25M net-new** (gross 4.13M; 54% articles, 34% wire, 45% MADLAD-dup) | 🚩 ~10–25× below est |
| fineweb-2 hat | ~300MB raw, *heavy overlap expected* | raw 159.29M, **net-new 108.49M** | 🚩 refutes overlap claim |
| Bloom hat (BY/BY-SA) | ~50–100k | pending gated access | deferred |
| Federal PDFs (CDC/USCIS/IRS/…) | ~1–5M | not measured in J0 (→ J2) | estimate |
| IRS Pub 850 / CFPB glossary | ~80–160k | not measured in J0 (→ J2/J3) | estimate |
| Bib La 1985 | ~1M | not measured in J0 (→ J4) | estimate (cap ≤2%) |
| Konstitisyon 1987 | ~20–60k | not measured in J0 (→ J4) | estimate |
| Storybooks Haiti | ~10–15k | not measured in J0 (→ J4) | estimate |
| Tatoeba / disaster SMS | negligible | — | seed/eval only |

## 5. What this means for v0.2

The measured net-new splits into two piles that serve **opposite** goals:

**Pile A — authored / register (the stated v0.2 thesis), small.**
- VOA authored journalism: **~2.25M**
- federal PDFs (health/legal/tax) + Bib La + Konstitisyon + Bloom + Storybooks: low single-digit millions combined (estimates; translation/religious/children's registers)

This pile is only a few million tokens, so it **cannot** triple the authored share to 30% or reach 150–180M. What it *does* buy is real and is exactly what v0.1 lacks: **register diversity** — journalism, health, legal, tax, children's registers the model has never seen — plus a genuine **authored-journalism eval axis** (`authored_eval_v2` from a held-out VOA temporal slice), which also closes the standing fertility-report translated-vs-authored TODO.

**Pile B — raw volume (not in the thesis), large and surprising.**
- fineweb-2 hat survivors: **~108.49M net-new kreyol-bpe** (~132.21M o200k) — nearly double the whole v0.1 corpus.

Model C v0 is **data-limited** (112M unique tokens, trained ~6.7 epochs). Pile B would roughly triple the unique-token budget (112M → ~220M+), letting a next Model C train far longer before repeating — a real scale win the survey wrote off. **But** it is more of the same translationese web-crawl register: dropping it into the corpus raw would push web-crawl dominance from ~92% toward ~96% and *lower* the authored share (8% → ~4–5%). It serves model *scale*, not corpus *quality*, and the two goals fight over it.

The clean way to hold both: ingest everything **register-tagged**, and drive the authored/register emphasis through the **training-mix weights** (upweight authored, cap web-crawl) rather than through what is physically in the corpus — the Micro-fleet Q2 (authored upweighting) knob. Caveat: even with upweighting, a ~2.5M authored pool can only be pushed to a high mix share by **repeating** it many times per epoch (overfit risk), so a true 30% authored target still needs a bigger authored source (permission-route).

### Recommendation

Per the gate, J0 **stops here and reports** rather than auto-proceeding on the old premise. The decision is the user's. The honest options:

1. **Build a register-tagged v0.2, control quality via the mix (recommended).** Ingest VOA + federal + small authored wins **and** fineweb-2's ~108M, every doc tagged by origin/register. The corpus then serves *both* a bigger data-limited Model C (Pile B volume) and a register/authored emphasis (Pile A, upweighted at train time). Report the raw composition honestly (authored ~4–5% raw) and the target mix weights — do not claim 30%-by-composition.
2. **Register-only v0.2 (quality purist).** Ingest Pile A only; **exclude/cap fineweb-2** to avoid diluting the register. ~117–120M unique, authored ~10–12%, maximal register signal per token. Forgoes the volume win.
3. **Widen the rights net first.** Revisit PERMISSION-ROUTE / contested sources (Healthy Roads, AKA, Ayibopost, NYC/Boston, Florida, MIT-Haiti) — the only path that actually reaches ~30% *authored* — before committing.
4. **Defer v0.2.** Hold at v0.1 if neither the volume nor the register value justifies the effort now.

The mechanical gate (VOA net-new < 5M) is genuinely tripped and the authored-unlock thesis is refuted; but fineweb-2 reframes the question from *"is there enough authored data?"* (no) to *"do we want a bigger web-crawl base and/or a register-diverse tail?"* (a design choice). That choice is why this stops for a human decision.

## Reproduce

```bash
cd ml && uv sync
uv run python -m corpus.corpus_index build          # near-dup index over v0.1
uv run python -m corpus.j0_scoping voa --n 200       # VOA stratified sample
uv run python -m corpus.j0_scoping overlap           # VOA↔v0.1 MinHash overlap
uv run python -m corpus.j0_scoping fineweb           # fineweb-2 net-new
uv run python -m corpus.j0_scoping bloom             # after HF gate accepted
uv run python -m corpus.report_v0_2_scoping          # this report
```

Sitemap, sample, overlap, and fineweb numbers are pinned in the sidecars under `data/interim/j0/`. Nothing under `ml/data/` is committed.
